title: Windows Security, Permissions, Encryption, and Recovery
description: A Windows laptop holds sensitive client data, uses shared folders, has inherited permissions, a lost recovery key, disabled protections, and a user who cannot complete an accessible sign-in flow. Learners cumulatively produce Windows endpoint security and recovery baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Configure standard and administrative accounts, UAC, Windows Hello, MFA-capable identity, lock, session, sign-in options, recovery, and accessible alternatives.
Reason about users, groups, ownership, inheritance, explicit allow and deny, effective NTFS and share access, elevation, and least privilege.
Plan BitLocker scope, TPM use, startup protection, recovery-key custody, escrow, suspension, hardware change, backup, verification, and disposition.
